title: Dense Peruvian Black Beans Stew
description: A thick, fatty stew made with fermented black beans and a salt crust finish, ideal as a hangover cure and macrobiotic-friendly meal.

ingredients.name: fermented black beans
ingredients.notes: soaked in water for 12 hours before cooking

instructions: Rinse the fermented black beans under cold water and drain.
In a large pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and minced garlic to the pot and sauté until translucent.
Stir in the smoked paprika, cumin seeds, and dried oregano, cooking until fragrant.
Add the diced tomatoes and cook for 3 minutes, letting them soften.
Pour in the vegetable broth and bring the mixture to a gentle boil.
Add the drained black beans to the pot, reduce heat, and simmer uncovered for 45 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Season the stew with sea salt to taste, then stir and taste again for balance.
Let the stew cool slightly, then transfer to a ceramic baking dish.
Cover the dish with a thick layer of sea salt, creating a salt crust.
Bake the salt-crusted stew in a preheated oven at 180°C for 30 minutes.
Remove from the oven, break the salt crust, and serve hot with fresh cilantro and lime wedges.
